name: Physics of Soft matter, Interfaces & Complex Systems
tagline: The universe blinked. We took notes.
welcome: Welcome to the Lab of Soft Matter, Interfaces, and Complex Systems. We explore the fascinating physics at the intersection of biology, fluid dynamics, and micro-scale engineering.

news.title: New publication in Physical Review Letters
news.description: Our work on droplet dynamics in microfluidic channels has been published.

news.title: Welcome to our new Postdoc!
news.description: Dr. Sarah Miller joins the lab to work on cellular biophysics.

news.title: Grant Awarded
news.description: The lab has received a new grant from ANRF for microfluidic sensors.

researchAreas.title: Cellular & Subcellular Biophysics
researchAreas.description: Investigating the mechanical properties and dynamics of cells and their internal components.

researchAreas.title: Droplet Microfluidics
researchAreas.description: Studying the formation, manipulation, and applications of droplets in micro-scale environments.

researchAreas.title: Microfluidic Sensors
researchAreas.description: Developing novel sensors for high-sensitivity detection in biological and chemical systems.

publications.title: Dynamics of Droplet Coalescence in Microfluidic Channels

pi.name: Dr. Joydip Chaudhuri
pi.bio: Assistant Professor at IIT Kharagpur. Research interests include soft matter, biophysics, and microfluidics.

projects.title: Cell Mechanics and Dynamics
projects.description: Investigating how cells respond to mechanical stress and the role of the cytoskeleton in maintaining structural integrity.

projects.title: High-Throughput Droplet Sorting
projects.description: Developed a novel microfluidic device for sorting droplets based on their fluorescence intensity at high speeds.
